course_description.overview: Out-and-back ride through western Cuba starting in Viñales Valley (Pinar del Río), passing towns in northern Pinar del Río, mountainous Minas de Matahambre region, ending at Cayo Jutías beach in Los Colorados archipelago[1].

key_segments.name: Cayo Jutías Finish
key_segments.description: Beach destination known for starfish and pink sunsets[1]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Moderate rolling terrain with 740m elevation gain over 115km on vintage bikes pre-1987, emphasizing cultural adventure over speed[1].

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Havana; travel west to Viñales Valley start (Caimito linked to founder's history); ground transport required for remote western Cuba[1].
logistics.accommodation: Local options in Viñales Valley and Cayo Jutías; Cuban hospitality emphasized[1].
logistics.registration_details: Open to all levels; check Eroica.cc for entries; vintage bike requirement (pre-1987 except helmets)[1].
logistics.bike_logistics: Mandatory vintage bicycles, clothing, accessories pre-1987 (helmets modern OK); ship to Cuba challenging due to logistics[1].
logistics.local_considerations: Unique Caribbean Eroica; combines cycling with Cuban culture; visa, currency restrictions, limited infrastructure in rural areas[1].

final_verdict.one_liner: Vintage cycling immersion in Cuba's stunning landscapes and culture.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Vintage bike enthusiasts seeking unique adventure and hospitality in the Caribbean.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the notable cultural and scenic experience despite logistics hurdles.
pro_tips: Source vintage bike early and plan shipping to Cuba
Visit Viñales UNESCO sites pre/post ride
Embrace non-competitive spirit; open to all
Prepare for rural Cuba: cash, basic Spanish, flexibility
